items.description: Conduct extended literature review focusing on post-2023 advances in Hadamard matrix construction, Turyn-type sequences, propus arrays, and GP arrays via web search and Semantic Scholar
items.acceptance_criteria: At least 10 NEW relevant papers added to sources.bib (beyond the existing 32 entries) with valid BibTeX. Must include: (1) Djokovic 2025 (arXiv:2508.17141) on orthogonal designs and symmetric Hadamard matrices, (2) London & Kotsireas 2025 on new Turyn-type sequences (doi:10.1007/s12095-025-00829-z), (3) Shen & Zhang 2024 on GS sequences with special structures (doi:10.3390/math12040530), (4) Di Matteo, Djokovic & Kotsireas 2015 on symmetric H(116) and H(172) via propus-GP arrays (arXiv:1503.04226), (5) Balonin & Seberry 2014/2016 on propus construction for symmetric Hadamard matrices, (6) at least 5 additional sources on: negacirculant Hadamard constructions, Turyn-type sequence generalizations, SageMath Hadamard database updates (Cati-Pasechnik v2 2025), modular Hadamard lifting techniques, and GPU-accelerated combinatorial search. Fix all 7 erroneous citations identified in peer_review.md and remove the 2 fabricated entries (bright2019applying, spence1977sds)

items.description: Delegate concept-tree exploration: map unexplored construction paths for H(668) including propus arrays, GP arrays, negacirculant blocks, and Turyn-type sequences
items.acceptance_criteria: At least 5 NEW concept nodes added to results/concept_evolve/tree/ (numbered 013+), each with concept.json, literature.json, and README.md. Must cover: (1) Propus array construction (A,B,C,D where B=C symmetric) for symmetric H(668), (2) GP (Goethals-Pasechnik) array with mixed symmetric/skew base blocks, (3) Negacirculant block construction (two-circulant core with negacirculant), (4) Turyn-type sequences TT(167) with lengths (167,167,167,166), (5) Modular lifting: can Eliahou's 64-modular H(668) be lifted to an exact H(668)? Each node must include feasibility_score (0-10), adjacency links to existing nodes, and at least 2 literature references. Updated index.json and adjacency.json reflecting the expanded tree

items.description: Deep algebraic analysis of Z_167 to identify exploitable structure missed by prior work: Jacobi sums, Gauss periods, index-2 and index-83 subgroups, and quadratic form representations of 167
items.acceptance_criteria: Document results/analysis/algebraic_deep_dive.md containing: (1) Complete table of Gauss periods eta_i for Z_167 with both 2-class and 83-class decompositions, (2) All Jacobi sum values J(chi^a, chi^b) for the characters of Z_167* and their absolute values, (3) Explicit quadratic form representations: enumerate all ways 668 = a^2+b^2+c^2+d^2 as sums of 4 squares (these constrain possible row sums of the 4 circulant blocks), (4) Analysis of the 2-element subgroup {1,166} and the index-2 subgroup (QR) -- can skew-symmetric sequences exploit the QR/QNR partition differently than symmetric ones?, (5) Comparison of Z_167* structure with Z_163* (phi=162=2*81, has 3-Sylow) and Z_173* (phi=172=4*43, has 4-class cyclotomic) to explain why 167 is harder

items.description: Analyze all Turyn-type sequence constructions TT(n) for n near 167 to identify transferable patterns
items.acceptance_criteria: Document results/analysis/turyn_analysis.md with: (1) Survey of known TT(n) for n in {157,163,167,173,179} from London-Kotsireas 2025 and Djokovic-Kotsireas tables, (2) If TT(167) is known, document the sequences -- these directly yield H(668) via the Goethals-Seidel array, (3) If TT(167) is NOT known, analyze the algebraic constraints that prevent known construction methods, (4) For each known TT(n) with n near 167, extract the 'template' (which positions are +1/-1 relative to QR/QNR partition) and test if similar templates work at n=167. Must cite at least 3 papers on Turyn-type sequences

items.description: Audit and fix all data integrity issues from peer review: correct experiment log, fix CSV filename, reconcile paper claims with actual data
items.acceptance_criteria: (1) results/experiments/experiment_log.json updated with accurate evaluation counts and metrics matching all actual search runs, (2) hadamard_668.csv renamed to best_candidate_668.csv OR hadamard_668.csv header/README clearly states it is a near-miss (HH^T != 668I), (3) All 7 partially-correct BibTeX entries in sources.bib corrected per peer_review.md Section 3 requirements, (4) 2 fabricated entries removed, (5) 3 unused entries removed, (6) Document all changes in results/review/integrity_fixes.md

items.description: Enumerate all valid row-sum quadruples and map each to algebraic constraints on the four GS sequences
items.acceptance_criteria: Document results/analysis/rowsum_constraints.md containing: (1) Complete list of all (r1,r2,r3,r4) with r_i odd, r1^2+r2^2+r3^2+r4^2=668, and each r_i in [-167,167] (the existing 10 decompositions from targeted_search.py used only positive values; include negative and mixed-sign), (2) For each quadruple, the exact number of +1 and -1 entries required in each sequence, (3) DFT constraint analysis: for each row sum quadruple, what are the values of a_hat(0), b_hat(0), c_hat(0), d_hat(0) and how does this constrain the PSD at k=0?, (4) Identification of the most 'balanced' quadruples (closest to equal block sizes) as these minimize spectral variance and should be searched first

items.description: Implement GPU-accelerated PSD evaluation using CuPy or PyTorch for 100-1000x speedup over CPU NumPy
items.acceptance_criteria: Script results/gpu_search.py that: (1) Uses CuPy or PyTorch to batch-evaluate PSD for thousands of candidate sequence modifications simultaneously, (2) Achieves at least 1M PSD evaluations/second (vs ~31K/s on CPU), (3) Includes benchmark comparing CPU vs GPU throughput on identical workloads, (4) Implements batched parallel flip evaluation: given a base sequence, evaluate all 167 possible single-flip modifications in parallel, (5) Falls back gracefully to CPU NumPy if no GPU available. If GPU is not available in the environment, implement the batched-flip strategy on CPU using vectorized NumPy operations for at least 10x speedup

items.description: Implement propus array construction and verification as an alternative to standard Goethals-Seidel
items.acceptance_criteria: Module results/propus_construction.py with functions: (1) propus_array(A,B,C,D) building the 668x668 matrix from the propus array [[A,B,C,D],[C,D,-A,-B],[B,-A,-D,C],[D,-C,B,-A]] where A,B,C,D are 167x167 circulant matrices, (2) propus_psd_condition(a,b,c,d) computing the modified PSD condition for propus (which differs from GS), (3) verify_propus_hadamard(H) checking HH^T=668I, (4) Validation on a known propus-Hadamard matrix from Balonin-Seberry (e.g., order 92 or 172 from Di Matteo et al.). The propus construction restricts to B=C (symmetric) which halves certain degrees of freedom but produces SYMMETRIC Hadamard matrices

items.description: Implement Turyn-type sequence search framework for TT(167) with lengths (167,167,167,166)
items.acceptance_criteria: Script results/turyn_search.py implementing: (1) TT(n) framework: four +/-1 sequences X,Y,Z of length n and W of length n-1 satisfying N_X(s)+N_Y(s)+N_Z(s)+N_W(s)=0 for all s>0 where N_A(s) is the autocorrelation of A at shift s, (2) Conversion from TT(167) to H(668) via the standard embedding into GS array, (3) DFT-based fast autocorrelation checking, (4) SA search with the TT constraint structure (3 sequences of length 167 + 1 of length 166), (5) Validation by finding TT(n) for at least one known small case (e.g., TT(11) or TT(23)). The TT framework adds the constraint that W has length n-1, which changes the search landscape compared to standard GS

items.description: Delegate subagent to profile the convergence barrier: characterize WHY all SA methods plateau at L2~390K
items.acceptance_criteria: Report results/analysis/barrier_characterization.md with: (1) Frequency-resolved analysis: at the L2~390K plateau, which specific frequencies k have the largest PSD deviations? Are they random or structured (e.g., related to QR/QNR)?, (2) Correlation analysis: when SA flips position j to reduce PSD at frequency k, which other frequencies worsen and by how much? Compute the full 167x166 'flip impact matrix', (3) Entropy analysis: at the plateau, how many positions are 'frozen' (never flip in the last 10% of iterations) vs 'active'?, (4) Basin comparison: compare 5+ distinct local minima from multi-start SA -- do they share common subsequences or structural motifs?, (5) Spectral gap estimate: compute the cost function Hessian (or finite-difference approximation) at a local minimum to estimate the barrier height for escaping

items.description: Implement mixed symmetric/skew base block search following Djokovic-Kotsireas 2018 framework for GS difference families
items.acceptance_criteria: Script results/mixed_gs_search.py implementing: (1) Classification of all 2^4=16 combinations of symmetric/skew assignments for the 4 GS base blocks, (2) For skew block: a[0]=1 and a[j]=-a[p-j] for j>0 (83 free variables instead of 84 for symmetric), (3) For symmetric block: a[j]=a[p-j] for all j (84 free variables), (4) Separate SA search for each of the 16 type combinations, with at least 10M iterations each, (5) The all-symmetric case (Williamson) and all-skew case should be searched but emphasis on mixed types (e.g., 2 symmetric + 2 skew, 3 symmetric + 1 skew) as these are the novel search directions from Djokovic-Kotsireas 2018, (6) Log the best PSD deviation per type combination. Results must show whether any mixed type achieves lower PSD deviation than the pure-symmetric (Williamson) approach which plateaued at L2~450K

items.description: Implement modular lifting strategy: attempt to lift Eliahou's 64-modular H(668) to an exact Hadamard matrix
items.acceptance_criteria: Script results/modular_lifting.py that: (1) Constructs the 64-modular Hadamard matrix M of order 668 following Eliahou 2025 (MM^T = 668I mod 64), (2) Extracts the 'error matrix' E = MM^T - 668I (all entries divisible by 64), (3) Attempts local search: starting from M, flip entries to reduce max|E_ij| while maintaining the +/-1 constraint, (4) Implements a greedy reduction algorithm: for each off-diagonal (i,j) with E_ij != 0, identify which entry flips reduce |E_ij| without increasing the maximum elsewhere, (5) Documents whether the 64-modular solution is 'close' to exact (how many off-diagonal entries are non-zero? what is their distribution?). This is a novel approach: instead of searching from scratch, start from the best known near-solution and locally repair it

items.description: Implement large-neighborhood search (LNS) with DFT-guided destruction and reconstruction to escape SA local minima
items.acceptance_criteria: Script results/lns_search.py implementing: (1) Start from the best known local minimum (the Legendre baseline or best SA result), (2) DESTROY phase: randomly zero out a contiguous block of k positions in one or more sequences (k=10,20,30,...,80), (3) RECONSTRUCT phase: use greedy or branch-and-bound to fill in the destroyed positions optimally given the remaining fixed positions (this is a sub-problem with 2^k possibilities but prunable via DFT bounds), (4) Accept/reject the reconstructed solution via SA criterion, (5) Adaptive destruction size: increase k when stuck, decrease when making progress, (6) Run for at least 10^6 destroy-reconstruct cycles. The key insight: LNS can escape basins that single-flip SA cannot because it changes many positions simultaneously in a structured way

items.description: Delegate exploration of cross-construction transfer: extract successful GS sequences for p=163 and p=173 from SageMath and analyze what makes them work
items.acceptance_criteria: Updated concept tree with new bridge nodes linking p=163, p=167, p=173 constructions. Report results/analysis/neighbor_prime_transfer.md containing: (1) The actual 4 GS sequences for H(652) (p=163) extracted from SageMath database, (2) The actual 4 GS sequences for H(692) (p=173) from SageMath, (3) For each: the QR/QNR partition structure of the support sets, the row sums, the PSD profile, and whether the sequences are symmetric/skew/neither, (4) Statistical comparison: how do the successful sequences at p=163,173 differ from Legendre? What fraction of positions differ from the Legendre baseline?, (5) Template transfer attempt: map the QR-relative structure from p=163/173 solutions onto Z_167 and evaluate PSD -- does the 'shape' of the solution transfer even if the exact sequence doesn't?

items.description: Implement two-phase search: coarse global search via compressed representation followed by fine local search
items.acceptance_criteria: Script results/twophase_search.py implementing: (1) COARSE PHASE: represent each of the 4 sequences by its 83-orbit membership vector (83 binary variables per sequence, 332 total) -- each orbit is a pair {j, p-j} that must have the same value, (2) Run aggressive SA/genetic algorithm over this 332-dimensional space for 10^8+ iterations, (3) FINE PHASE: take the best orbit-level solutions from coarse phase, break the orbit constraint, and run unconstrained SA on the full 668-dimensional space starting from each coarse solution, (4) Interleave coarse and fine phases: periodically re-coarsen the best fine solutions and continue coarse search from the new starting points, (5) Report whether the coarse-to-fine transition reveals better solutions than either phase alone. Rationale: the orbit-level representation smooths the landscape, making global features visible

items.description: Implement SAT encoding of the SDS existence problem for v=167 with symmetry breaking from Z_167* multiplier group
items.acceptance_criteria: Script results/sat_encoding.py that: (1) Creates 4*167=668 Boolean variables x_{i,j} (block i, position j), (2) Encodes the PSD=668 condition as a system of 166 quadratic constraints (one per non-zero frequency), (3) Converts quadratic constraints to CNF via standard techniques (Tseitin transformation or pseudo-Boolean encoding), (4) Adds symmetry-breaking clauses from the order-2 automorphism j -> -j mod 167 and the primitive root g=5 multiplier, (5) Outputs DIMACS CNF file results/hadamard_668.cnf, (6) If pysat or a SAT solver is available, run with a timeout of 1 hour and report the outcome (SAT/UNSAT/TIMEOUT), (7) Reports the clause count and variable count of the encoding. Even if the SAT solver times out, the encoding itself is a valuable deliverable that can be run on dedicated hardware

items.description: Run systematic head-to-head experiments: all new methods (items 007-016) benchmarked with identical compute budgets
items.acceptance_criteria: Updated results/experiments/experiment_log.json with entries for each new method containing: (1) method name, (2) parameter settings (temperature schedule, destruction size, etc.), (3) wall-clock runtime (minimum 30 minutes per method), (4) number of PSD evaluations, (5) best L2 cost achieved, (6) best Linf achieved, (7) whether solution H(668) was found. Must include at minimum: GPU/batched SA, propus search, Turyn search, mixed sym/skew GS, modular lifting, LNS, two-phase coarse-fine, and SAT encoding. Each method gets equal wall-clock time for fair comparison

items.description: If H(668) found: verify rigorously and export to hadamard_668.csv
items.acceptance_criteria: If a candidate H is found: (1) verify HH^T = 668*I EXACTLY using Python integer arithmetic (not floating point), (2) verify all entries are exactly +1 or -1, (3) verify shape is 668x668, (4) export to hadamard_668.csv as comma-separated +1/-1 values (668 rows, 668 columns, no header), (5) record the construction method, parameters, and the 4 generating sequences in results/solution_sequences.npz, (6) verify that the generating sequences satisfy PSD(k)=668 for all non-zero k using exact integer DFT, (7) create a second independent verification using SageMath's matrix verification if available. This would be a major mathematical result -- triple-check everything

items.description: If H(668) NOT found: document enhanced negative results with quantitative comparison against all new approaches
items.acceptance_criteria: Updated results/experiments/negative_results.md with: (1) Extended comparison table including all new methods from Phase 3, (2) For each method: best L2, best Linf, number of evaluations, convergence rate (iterations to reach 90% of final quality), (3) Statistical analysis across 10+ random restarts per method (mean, std, min of best L2), (4) Landscape comparison: for the 3 best methods, overlay their convergence curves on a single plot, (5) Identification of the single most promising method for future large-scale compute investment, (6) Estimation of compute budget (GPU-hours) needed for each method to have >50% chance of success based on observed convergence rates. All data must be reproducible from scripts in results/

items.description: Delegate evaluation synthesis: compare all results (old + new) against prior literature, especially Djokovic-Kotsireas mixed-type results and Eliahou's 64-modular bound
items.acceptance_criteria: Updated results/experiments/comparative_analysis.md with: (1) Table comparing our mixed sym/skew results against Djokovic-Kotsireas 2018 results for v<50, (2) Analysis of whether our modular lifting improved on Eliahou's 64-modular bound (did we get closer to modulus 0?), (3) Comparison of our TT(167) search against known TT(n) existence table from London-Kotsireas 2025, (4) Assessment of which construction framework (GS/propus/TT/SAT) explored the most novel territory vs. prior computational work, (5) Clear statement of our contributions beyond the previous rubric v1.0 results

items.description: Generate publication-quality figures addressing all peer review criticisms
items.acceptance_criteria: Updated figures/ directory with: (1) figures/method_comparison_v2.png -- extended bar chart including all new methods with error bars from multiple restarts, (2) figures/convergence_all_methods.png -- convergence curves for all methods on a single log-log plot with consistent styling, (3) figures/barrier_heatmap.png -- frequency x position heatmap from barrier characterization (item_010), (4) figures/concept_tree_v2.png -- visual graph of the expanded concept tree (17+ nodes) with color-coded feasibility scores, (5) All figures use consistent font sizes (>=10pt), publication-quality colormaps (viridis/cividis, not jet), proper LaTeX axis labels, and legends. Address all figure quality criticisms from peer_review.md

items.description: Write revised research paper addressing all peer review revision requirements
items.acceptance_criteria: Updated research_paper.tex with: (1) Table 1 corrected to match actual experiment_log.json data (including new Phase 3-4 experiments), (2) All 7 erroneous citations fixed per peer_review.md, (3) 2 fabricated citations replaced with verified alternatives, (4) Numba citation corrected to Lam et al. 2015, (5) New results section covering Phase 3 novel approaches, (6) Updated figures (from item_021) integrated, (7) Discussion section enhanced with barrier characterization analysis and neighbor prime transfer insights, (8) If H(668) found: complete rewrite of abstract and conclusion announcing the result. Compiles without errors via pdflatex+bibtex

items.description: Finalize sources.bib: merge new references, verify all entries, ensure every citation has a corresponding entry
items.acceptance_criteria: File sources.bib containing: (1) All original valid entries (25 verified from peer_review.md), (2) At least 10 new entries from item_001 literature review, (3) Total >= 35 properly formatted BibTeX entries, (4) Every \cite{} in research_paper.tex has a corresponding entry, (5) No unused entries (every entry cited at least once), (6) All DOIs verified to resolve to the correct paper (spot-check at least 5), (7) No duplicate entries. Validation: bibtex compiles without warnings

items.description: Final concept tree synthesis: update all nodes with Phase 3-4 experimental outcomes and add walk paths documenting the complete research trajectory
items.acceptance_criteria: Updated results/concept_evolve/tree/ where: (1) All 17+ concept nodes have updated feasibility_score reflecting Phase 3-4 experiments, (2) Each new node (013-017+) has status: tested/dead_end/promising/inconclusive, (3) At least 3 new walk_paths added to walk_paths.json documenting the research trajectory from this rubric v2.0 (e.g., 'modular_lifting_path', 'mixed_gs_path', 'turyn_path'), (4) steering_notes.md updated with revised priority ordering based on experimental results, (5) summary.txt updated with final node/edge/path counts

items.description: Deliver final CSV output and verify complete repository integrity
items.acceptance_criteria: Repository contains: (1) hadamard_668.csv in repo root -- either an exact Hadamard matrix (verify HH^T=668I via integer arithmetic) OR the best near-miss matrix with clear documentation that it is NOT exact, (2) All Python scripts in results/ are importable without errors (test: python -c 'import results.hadamard_core'), (3) figures/ contains all generated plots (minimum 9 figures), (4) sources.bib is complete and valid, (5) research_paper.tex compiles without errors, (6) results/experiments/experiment_log.json has >= 12 experiment entries with accurate data, (7) results/concept_evolve/tree/ has >= 17 concept nodes, (8) README.md updated with v2.0 project summary describing both the original and extended research, (9) requirements.txt or pyproject.toml added listing all Python dependencies (numpy, numba, matplotlib, and any new ones)

items.description: Delegate final independent review: verify all new results, check reproducibility, and assess whether novel approaches genuinely advanced the state of knowledge
items.acceptance_criteria: Review report in results/review/final_review_v2.md containing: (1) Verification that all new scripts (items 007-016) execute without errors, (2) Spot-check reproduction of at least 3 experimental results from experiment_log.json, (3) Assessment of whether any new method achieved a strictly better result than the Legendre baseline's Linf=4, (4) Check that all new citations in sources.bib are verified (no fabricated entries), (5) Comparison of v1.0 vs v2.0 rubric outcomes: what did the new approaches reveal that was unknown before?, (6) Final recommendation: does the extended investigation constitute a genuine contribution? Identify the single most important new finding. Review must be performed by a separate subagent session for independence
